core_meaning: Simple past tense used in literary and formal written narratives to express completed actions.
primary_communicative_fn: Narrating past events in written literature, historical accounts, and formal writing
when_to_use: Use in written French, literature, fairy tales, historical narratives, and formal written texts. In spoken French, passé composé is used instead. The passé simple is immediately recognizable as the literary past tense.
pattern: -er verbs: je -ai, tu -as, il -a, nous -âmes, vous -âtes, ils -èrent | -ir/-re verbs: je -is, tu -is, il -it, nous -îmes, vous -îtes, ils -irent
key_variations: The passé simple is formed by adding endings to the infinitive stem (dropping -er/-ir/-re). Regular -er verbs: parlai, parlas, parla, parlâmes, parlâtes, parlèrent. Regular -ir verbs: finis, finis, finit, finîmes, finîtes, finirent. Regular -re verbs: vendis, vendis, vendit, vendîmes, vendîtes, vendirent.
essential_vocabulary_slots: narration: raconter, décrire, expliquer, montrer | actions: arriver, partir, dire, voir, faire | adverbs: soudain, ensuite, enfin, puis
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing passé simple endings with present tense; forgetting accent marks in nous/vous forms (âmes, âtes, îmes, îtes); using passé simple in spoken French where passé composé is required; not recognizing passé simple in reading comprehension
chunk_compatibility: Narrative expressions: Il arriva que..., Soudain il dit..., Dès qu'il vit...
key_forms: parlai, finis, vendis, parla, finit, vendit
essential_vocabulary: arriver, partir, dire, voir, faire, raconter, décrire, soudain
why_it_matters: Passé simple is foundational for reading literature and understanding formal French narratives; essential for recognizing verb tenses in literary contexts.
common_mistakes: Wrong accent marks on nous/vous; using in speech instead of writing; confusing with passé composé in reading.

structure_label: passé simple des verbes irréguliers courants
core_meaning: Simple past tense of high-frequency irregular verbs in literary and formal narratives.
primary_communicative_fn: Narrating past events with high-frequency irregular verbs in literary contexts
when_to_use: Use in written narratives with common irregular verbs: être, avoir, faire, dire, pouvoir, vouloir, devoir, voir, prendre, mettre, venir, savoir. These irregular forms must be memorized as they do not follow regular patterns.
pattern: être: fus, fus, fut, fûmes, fûtes, furent | avoir: eus, eus, eut, eûmes, eûtes, eurent | faire: fis, fis, fit, fîmes, fîtes, firent | dire: dis, dis, dit, dîmes, dîtes, dirent | voir: vis, vis, vit, vîmes, vîtes, virent | prendre: pris, pris, prit, prîmes, prîtes, prirent
key_variations: être: fus (I was), fut (he was), fûmes (we were), fûtes (you were), furent (they were) | avoir: eus (I had), eut (he had), eûmes (we had), eûtes (you had), eurent (they had) | These forms appear frequently in literature and must be memorized.
essential_vocabulary_slots: irregular verbs: être, avoir, faire, dire, voir, prendre, mettre, venir, savoir, pouvoir, devoir, vouloir | auxiliary: être, avoir
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing irregular passé simple forms with present or passé composé; failing to memorize high-frequency irregular forms; mixing up être and avoir passé simple forms
chunk_compatibility: Narrative combinations: Il fut..., Elle eut..., Ils firent..., On vit...
key_forms: fus, fut, furent, eus, eut, eurent, fis, fit, firent
essential_vocabulary: être, avoir, faire, dire, voir, prendre, venir, savoir
why_it_matters: Common irregular passé simple forms are essential for reading classic literature and understanding literary narratives.
common_mistakes: Not memorizing high-frequency irregular forms; confusing with présent; using in speech instead of writing.

core_meaning: Pluperfect (past perfect) expressing an action completed before another past action.
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing an action that occurred before another past action, creating narrative sequence
when_to_use: Use when describing an action that happened before another past action (both already completed). Common in narrative, reported speech, and when explaining what had happened before something else occurred.
pattern: Subject + conjugated avoir/être in imparfait + past participle | j'avais mangé, tu étais parti, il avait fini, nous avions compris, vous aviez vu, ils avaient pris
key_variations: Agreement: past participle agrees with auxiliary if être (je m'étais levé → je m'étais levée for feminine). Regular verbs: parler→avais parlé, finir→avais fini, vendre→avais vendu. Reflexive: s'était lavé, s'étaient promenées.
essential_vocabulary_slots: sequence markers: avant que, lorsque, quand, après que | background actions: already, previously, earlier, before | verbs: finir, accomplir, compléter, réussir
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing plus-que-parfait with passé composé (sequence of tenses); forgetting agreement with être auxiliary; using where imparfait would be appropriate; incorrect auxiliary selection
chunk_compatibility: Narrative sequences: Il avait déjà..., Quand elle fut arrivée, elle avait mangé..., Dès qu'il eut fini...
key_forms: j'avais mangé, j'étais parti, nous avions fini, elles s'étaient levées
essential_vocabulary: avais, était, avaient, étaient, mangé, parti, fini, compris
why_it_matters: Establishes chronological relationships in narratives; critical for expressing 'had already done' before another past event.
common_mistakes: Confusing with passé composé; forgetting agreement with être; wrong auxiliary choice.

core_meaning: Before doing something; infinitive construction for same-subject clauses expressing sequence.
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing that one action precedes another, sequencing events
when_to_use: Use avant de + infinitive when the subject of both clauses is the same. The infinitive action occurs before the main verb action. Very common in instructions, recommendations, and narrative sequences.
pattern: avant de + infinitif | avant de partir, avant de manger, avant de se coucher, avant de répondre, avant de finir
key_variations: Avant de contracts before vowel: avant d'entamer, avant d'arriver. Negative: avant de ne pas faire. With pronouns: avant de me le dire, avant de t'en aller. Compare with avant que + subjunctive (different subjects).
essential_vocabulary_slots: actions: partir, manger, dormir, travailler, répondre, agir | reflexive: se lever, se coucher, se brosser, se maquiller | movement: sortir, entrer, aller, venir
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing avant de (same subject) with avant que (different subjects); using finite verb instead of infinitive; forgetting the de before the infinitive
chunk_compatibility: Instructional phrases: Avant de partir..., Avant de manger..., Avant de te coucher..., Avant de commencer...
key_forms: avant de partir, avant d'arriver, avant de manger, avant de finir
essential_vocabulary: avant, de, partir, manger, dormir, se coucher, commencer, finir
why_it_matters: Essential for expressing sequence in same-subject contexts; prevents confusion with 'avant que' which requires subjunctive.
common_mistakes: Using finite verb instead of infinitive; confusing with 'avant que'; forgetting apostrophe before vowel.

core_meaning: After having done something; emphasizes completed action before main clause.
primary_communicative_fn: Emphasizing completion of a preceding action before the main clause
when_to_use: Use après avoir + past participle when the subject of both clauses is the same and you want to emphasize the completed nature of the preceding action. Common in written French, formal contexts, and literary narrative.
pattern: après avoir + past participle | après avoir mangé, après être parti, après avoir fini, après s'être levé, après avoir compris
key_variations: With être verbs (motion/state): après être parti, après être arrivée, après être tombé. Past participle does NOT agree with après avoir/être (exception to normal agreement rules). Reflexive: après s'être lavé, après s'être couchés.
essential_vocabulary_slots: completed actions: finir, manger, travailler, dormir, lire | movement: arriver, partir, sortir, entrer, venir | reflexive: se lever, se préparer, s'habiller
common_learner_mistakes: Adding unnecessary agreement after après avoir (incorrect); forgetting that être verbs use après être; confusing with avant de + infinitive
chunk_compatibility: Literary sequences: Après avoir mangé..., Après être parti..., Après s'être levé..., Après avoir compris...
key_forms: après avoir mangé, après être parti, après s'être lavé, après avoir fini
essential_vocabulary: après, avoir, être, mangé, parti, fini, levé, compris
why_it_matters: Emphasizes completed action in formal/literary contexts; common in written French for temporal sequencing.
common_mistakes: Over-agreeing past participle; confusing with 'avant de'; using wrong auxiliary.

structure_label: pronoms toniques (moi, toi, lui, elle, nous, vous, eux, elles)
core_meaning: Stressed/emphatic pronouns for emphasis, contrast, and after prepositions.
primary_communicative_fn: Emphasis, contrast, standalone use, and after prepositions
when_to_use: Use stressed pronouns for emphasis, after prepositions (à, de, pour, avec, chez), standalone responses, contrasts, and with -même forms. They provide emphasis that weak pronouns cannot.
pattern: moi / toi / lui / elle / nous / vous / eux / elles | après prépositions: pour moi, avec toi, sans eux | emphasis: moi, je pense que... | alone: qui est là? - moi!
key_variations: With -même: moi-même, toi-même, lui-même, nous-mêmes. With conjunction: moi qui..., toi qui.... Combined: c'est moi, c'est toi. In comparisons: il est plus grand que moi (not je).
essential_vocabulary_slots: prepositions: pour, contre, avec, sans, chez, vers, depuis, avant | comparisons: que moi, que toi, que lui | emphasis: même, aussi, particulièrement
common_learner_mistakes: Using weak pronouns (je, tu) after prepositions instead of stressed pronouns; confusing stressed pronouns with object pronouns; using subject pronoun where stressed required
chunk_compatibility: Emphasis patterns: C'est moi, Pour moi, Avec lui, Sans toi, Moi-même, Lui aussi
key_forms: moi, toi, lui, elle, nous, vous, eux, elles, moi-même, toi-même
essential_vocabulary: moi, toi, lui, elle, nous, vous, eux, elles, même, aussi
why_it_matters: Stressed pronouns are essential for emphasis, contrast, and after prepositions; incorrect usage is a common English-speaker error.
common_mistakes: Using subject pronouns after prepositions; confusing with weak pronouns; forgetting in comparisons.

structure_label: pronoms complément : me, te, se, nous, vous
core_meaning: Direct and indirect object pronouns replacing nouns as recipients of actions.
primary_communicative_fn: Replacing nouns as direct/indirect objects, essential for pronoun verb combinations
when_to_use: Use before the verb (in positive commands use him/her after verb) when the object is a person. me/te/se can be direct or indirect depending on verb. In imperative, pronouns follow with hyphen.
pattern: me/m' (me) / te/t' (you) / se/s' (himself/herself/itself/themselves) / nous (us) / vous (you plural/formal) | me voir, te parler, se lever, nous aider, vous coucher
key_variations: Before vowel: m', t', s'. Imperative affirmative: donne-le-moi, dis-lui, montre-nous. Imperative negative: ne me regarde pas, ne te plains pas. Position: before verb (je te vois), except affirmative imperative.
essential_vocabulary_slots: verbs with me/te: voir, regarder, parler, dire, donner, montrer, aider, attendre | reflexive: se lever, se laver, se coiffer, se brosser | imperative: donne, dis, montre
common_learner_mistakes: Forgetting pronoun position before verb; confusing direct/indirect object; not using apostrophe before vowel; using me/te where lui/leur needed
chunk_compatibility: Object patterns: Me voir, Te parler, Se lever, Nous aider, Vous regarder, Donne-moi, Dis-lui
key_forms: me, te, se, nous, vous, m', t', s'
essential_vocabulary: me, te, se, nous, vous, voir, parler, dire, donner, regarder, aider
why_it_matters: Essential for pronoun substitution and avoiding repetition; critical for natural French speech and imperative commands.
common_mistakes: Wrong position before verb; not eliding before vowel; confusing with indirect pronouns; wrong imperative order.

structure_label: double pronoms (me + y, me + en, etc.)
core_meaning: Combined pronouns with object + adverbial (y/en) for complex reference.
primary_communicative_fn: Using multiple pronouns together for efficiency and fluency
when_to_use: Use when combining an indirect object pronoun (me, te, lui, nous, vous, leur) with a place pronoun (y) or quantity pronoun (en). Order matters: me/te/se/nous/vous precede y/en.
pattern: me + y = me y | te + y = t'y | nous + y = nous y | me + en = m'en | te + en = t'en | nous + en = nous en | lui/leur + y = lui y | lui/leur + en = lui en
key_variations: Order 1: me/te/se/nous/vous + y/en (je m'y intéresse, je m'en souviens). Order 2: lui/leur + y/en (je lui y parle - rare). Imperative: donne-m'en, montre-m'y.
essential_vocabulary_slots: pronoun combinations: m'en, t'y, s'en, nous y, vous en | verbs: penser, souvenir, intéresser, méfier | imperative: donne, montre, dis, parle
common_learner_mistakes: Wrong pronoun order with lui/leur vs me/te; forgetting apostrophe before vowel; using wrong combinations; confusing y/en positions
chunk_compatibility: Double pronoun patterns: M'en parle, T'y intéresses, S'en souvient, Nous y allons, Donne-m'en, Montre-m'y
key_forms: m'en, t'y, s'en, nous y, vous en, lui y, lui en
essential_vocabulary: me, te, se, nous, vous, lui, y, en, penser, souvenir, intéresser
why_it_matters: Double pronouns are efficient and fluent in French; essential for avoiding repetitive phrasing.
common_mistakes: Wrong order; missing apostrophe; confusing with simple pronouns; rare lui y combinations.

structure_label: y et en comme pronoms adverbiaux
core_meaning: Y (there) and en (some/of it) as adverbial pronouns replacing places and quantities.
primary_communicative_fn: Replacing prepositional phrases with places and quantities/sources
when_to_use: Use y to replace à + noun or place (à Paris → j'y vais), en to replace de + noun or express quantity/origin (du pain → j'en veux). Both essential for pronoun substitution and avoiding repetition.
pattern: y = à + noun/place | en = de + noun/quantité | j'y vais (I go there), j'en viens (I come from there), j'y pense (I think about it), j'en ai besoin (I need some)
key_variations: Negative: je n'y vais pas, je n'en ai pas. With pronouns: je m'y intéresse, je m'en souviens. Imperative: va-y, mange-en. En never elides.
essential_vocabulary_slots: places: Paris, maison, école, bureau, plage | quantities: trois, beaucoup, assez, peu | verbs: penser, croire, avoir, parler
common_learner_mistakes: Using y for people (use lui/leur instead); forgetting en replaces both noun and de; not placing y/en in correct position; confusion between y and où
chunk_compatibility: Place and quantity patterns: J'y vais, J'en veux, N'y va pas, Mange-en, Je m'y intéresse, Je m'en souviens
key_forms: y, en, j'y vais, j'en veux, va-y, mange-en, ne m'y intéresse
essential_vocabulary: y, en, vais, veux, pense, souviens, ai, besoin, place, quantity
why_it_matters: Y and en are fundamental pronouns for expressing place and quantity; essential for fluent French conversation.
common_mistakes: Using y for people; misplacing y/en before verb; confusing with où; overusing y.

structure_label: ce qui, ce que, ce dont
core_meaning: Relative pronouns for unspecified antecedents expressing 'what' in general clauses.
primary_communicative_fn: Referring to general ideas, situations, or unnamed objects in subordinate clauses
when_to_use: Use ce qui/ce que/ce dont when the antecedent is unspecified, general, or the entire clause preceding. ce qui = subject, ce que = object, ce dont = with de verbs.
pattern: ce qui (subject) + verb | ce que (object) + verb | ce dont (with de verbs) + verb | ce qui m'intéresse | ce que je veux dire | ce dont j'ai besoin
key_variations: ce qui and ce que can be replaced by cela qui/que. ce dont is equivalent to ce que + de. Examples: dis-moi ce qui s'est passé, dis-moi ce que tu fais, dis-moi ce dont tu as besoin.
essential_vocabulary_slots: verbs with de: avoir besoin, parler, se méfier, douter | unknown objects: quelque chose, tout, rien | questions: quoi, que, qui
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing ce qui (subject) with ce que (object); forgetting to use ce dont with de verbs; using ce que where ce qui needed; incorrect verb conjugation
chunk_compatibility: Clause patterns: Ce qui m'intéresse, Ce que je veux, Ce dont j'ai besoin, Dis-moi ce qui..., Je sais ce que...
key_forms: ce qui, ce que, ce dont, ce à quoi
essential_vocabulary: ce, qui, que, dont, m'intéresse, veux, besoin, parle, souviens
why_it_matters: Essential for expressing 'what' when the antecedent is an entire idea rather than a specific noun.
common_mistakes: Confusing ce qui with ce que; missing ce dont; wrong function after pronoun.

core_meaning: Relative pronoun dont (of which/whom) replacing de + noun in relative clauses.
primary_communicative_fn: Replacing de + relative pronoun constructions for smoother sentences
when_to_use: Use dont to introduce relative clauses when the relative pronoun's function is de + noun (possession, origin, cause, or with certain verbs/adjectives). Equivalent to 'de qui' for people and 'duquel/de laquelle' for things.
pattern: dont + verb | dont + subject + verb | la femme dont je parle (the woman I'm talking about) | l'argent dont j'ai besoin (the money I need) | le pays dont je viens (the country I come from)
key_variations: Dont can mean: of whom (people), of which (things), whose (possession), from which (origin). It always precedes the verb. Word order: dont + subject + verb (not: dont + verb + subject).
essential_vocabulary_slots: verbs with de: parler, avoir besoin, se souvenir, se méfier, douter, rire, sourire | possession: le père dont je suis fier | origin: le village dont je viens
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing dont with que (dont replaces de + noun, not just noun); placing verb before subject after dont; using dont for direct objects; incorrect word order
chunk_compatibility: Possession patterns: Dont je suis fier, Dont j'ai besoin, Dont je parle, Dont je viens, Dont il se souvient
key_forms: dont je parle, dont j'ai besoin, dont je viens, dont elle se souvient
essential_vocabulary: dont, parle, besoin, viens, souviens, fier, parler, avoir besoin
why_it_matters: Dont is essential for expressing relationships with 'de' in relative clauses; prevents confusion with direct objects.
common_mistakes: Using dont for direct objects; wrong word order in subordinate clause; confusing with duquel.

core_meaning: Present subjunctive mood expressing subjectivity, doubt, desire, necessity, and emotion.
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing subjectivity, doubt, desire, necessity, and emotional judgment
when_to_use: Use after expressions of doubt (douter que), desire (vouloir que), necessity (il faut que), emotion (regretter que), and certain conjunctions (bien que, pour que). The subjunctive indicates the action is uncertain, desired, or hypothetical.
pattern: que + subjunctive: je parle → que je parle | regular -er: que je parle, que tu parles, qu'il parle, que nous parlions, que vous parliez, qu'ils parlent | regular -ir: que je finisse | regular -re: que je vende
key_variations: We remove -ent ending: -er verbs take -e, -es, -e, -ions, -iez, -ent. Note: subjunctive nous/vous forms have same endings as indicative imparfait. Irregular subjunctives: avoir→aie, être→sois, faire→fasse, aller→aille, pouvoir→puisse, vouloir→veuille.
essential_vocabulary_slots: conjunctions: pour que, afin que, avant que, sans que, bien que | expressions: il faut que, je veux que, je doute que, j'espère que (usually indicative)
common_learner_mistakes: Using indicative where subjunctive is required; forgetting irregular subjunctive forms; confusing subjunctive endings with indicative; using subjunctive after croire que (usually indicative)
chunk_compatibility: Subjunctive expressions: Il faut que..., Je veux que..., C'est important que..., Avant que..., Bien que...
key_forms: que je parle, que tu finisses, qu'il vende, que nous parlions, qu'elles finissent
essential_vocabulary: que, parle, finisse, vende, parlions, jouions, sois, aie, fasse
why_it_matters: Subjunctive is essential for expressing doubt, desire, and necessity; required after specific trigger expressions.
common_mistakes: Using indicative instead of subjunctive; missing trigger expression; wrong irregular form; confusing with conditional.

core_meaning: It is necessary that; expressing necessity and obligation with subjunctive.
when_to_use: Use il faut que + subjunctive to express necessity, obligation, or requirement. This is one of the most common subjunctive triggers. Alternative: il faut + infinitive when same subject (no que needed).
pattern: il faut que + subjunctive | il faut que je parte, il faut que tu fasses, il faut qu'il arrive, il faut que nous étudiions, il faut que vous mangiez
key_variations: Same subject alternative: il faut partir (no que). Past: il a fallu que + subjunctive. Conditional: il faudrait que + subjunctive. Negative: il ne faut pas que + subjunctive. With noun: il faut de la patience.
essential_vocabulary_slots: necessity: partir, travailler, étudier, comprendre, accepter | rejection: tricher, mentir, voler | acceptance: essayer, persévérer, continuer
common_learner_mistakes: Using infinitive when different subject (needs que + subjunctive); forgetting the que; using indicative instead of subjunctive
chunk_compatibility: Necessity patterns: Il faut que je parte, Il faut que tu travailles, Il faut qu'il comprenne, Il ne faut pas que...
key_forms: il faut que, il a fallu que, il faudrait que, il ne faut pas que
essential_vocabulary: il faut que, parte, fasses, arrive, étudiions, mangiez, comprenne, accepte
why_it_matters: Essential for expressing necessity and obligation; one of the most common subjunctive triggers.
common_mistakes: Using infinitive instead of subjunctive with different subjects; missing 'que'; confusing with 'il faut + infinitive'.

core_meaning: Before that; subjunctive after temporal conjunction for different-subject clauses.
primary_communicative_fn: Emphasizing the hypothetical or uncertain nature of a following action
when_to_use: Use avant que + subjunctive when two actions involve different subjects and you want to emphasize that the second action may or may not happen. Note: avant de + infinitive is used when subjects are the same.
pattern: avant que + subjunctive | avant que je parte, avant que tu arrives, avant qu'il finisse, avant que nous partions, avant que vous mangiez
key_variations: Subjunctive is required after avant que (unlike English 'before' can take indicative). Optional ne explétif in literary French after avant que: avant que je ne parte. Note: no de after avant que (unlike avant de).
essential_vocabulary_slots: actions: partir, arriver, finir, manger, dormir | same subject: use avant de + infinitive instead | urgency: immediately, quickly, soon
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing avant que (different subjects, subjunctive) with avant de (same subject, infinitive); using indicative after avant que; adding de after avant que
chunk_compatibility: Temporal patterns: Avant que je parte, Avant qu'il arrive, Avant que tu finisses, Avant que nous partions
key_forms: avant que, avant que je parte, avant qu'il arrive, avant que nous partions
essential_vocabulary: avant que, parte, arrives, finisse, partions, mangiez, arriving, leave
why_it_matters: Essential for temporal clauses with different subjects; prevents confusion with 'avant de + infinitive'.
common_mistakes: Using indicative instead of subjunctive; confusing with 'avant de'; adding 'de' after 'avant que'.

structure_label: accord des participe passés avec avoir
core_meaning: Past participle agreement with avoir when direct object precedes the verb.
primary_communicative_fn: Correct gender/number agreement when using compound tenses with avoir
when_to_use: Use avoir as auxiliary in compound tenses. Past participle agrees with the DIRECT OBJECT when it precedes the verb (before avoir). If no preceding object, no agreement. Objects after verb do not cause agreement.
pattern: Direct object precedes: l'ai vue (I saw her) [vue agrees with feminine la] | je les ai vus (I saw them masc) | Direct object follows: j'ai vu la fille (no agreement)
key_variations: When direct object pronoun (le, la, les) precedes the verb, agreement occurs. When en precedes, no agreement (en is considered indefinite). When pronoun is indirect object, no agreement with avoir. Examples: je les ai vues (feminine plural), je l'ai vu (masculine singular), j'en ai vu (no agreement).
essential_vocabulary_slots: preceding direct objects: le, la, les | pronouns: me, te, se, nous, vous (no agreement with avoir) | en: no agreement | verbs: voir, manger, finir, prendre, faire
common_learner_mistakes: Incorrectly adding agreement when object follows verb; over-agreeing with en; confusing direct/indirect objects for agreement; confusion between être (mandatory) vs avoir (conditional agreement)
chunk_compatibility: Agreement patterns: Je l'ai vue, Les livres que j'ai achetés, Les filles qu'il a vues, Elle l'a fait
key_forms: l'ai vue, les ai vus, l'ai vu, les ai vues
essential_vocabulary: avoir, le, la, les, vu, vue, vus, vues, achetés, faits
why_it_matters: Critical for correct written French; agreement only occurs with preceding direct objects, not with indirect or following objects.
common_mistakes: Adding agreement when object follows; over-agreeing with 'en'; confusing direct and indirect objects.
